title: Expand commit and verify tool responses to expose canonical workflow follow-up
summary: Strengthen the response contracts of workflow-driving tools so an agent following `.rules` can reliably determine whether a transaction is terminal or still requires explicit follow-up. This ticket focuses on `commit_if_verified`, `repo_commit`, and, if appropriate for consistency, `repo_verify`, so their responses expose canonical workflow cues such as current status, current phase, next action, and whether `ops_end_task` is still required.
outputs: Expanded response payloads for `commit_if_verified` and `repo_commit` that expose canonical workflow follow-up requirements
Consistent workflow-oriented response fields for commit/verify helpers where applicable
Regression tests proving that successful commit responses make non-terminal `committed` state and required next action explicit
acceptance_criteria: A successful `commit_if_verified` response makes it explicit that the transaction may remain non-terminal after commit and exposes the canonical next action.
A successful `repo_commit` response includes enough workflow-state information for an agent to determine whether explicit terminal closure is still required.
If `repo_verify` is updated for consistency, its response clearly signals the verified state and the next canonical workflow action.
Response payloads preserve existing success information such as commit SHA and commit message while adding machine-usable workflow guidance.
The added response fields do not contradict the canonical transaction state persisted in `tx_state`.
Tests fail if commit helpers again return success without exposing the remaining required terminal action when the transaction is still only `committed`.
tasks: Review the current return shapes of `commit_if_verified`, `repo_commit`, and `repo_verify` and identify which fields are insufficient for agent decision-making.
Define a minimal backward-compatible response contract that includes canonical workflow cues such as status, phase, next action, terminal flag, and follow-up requirement.
Implement the new response fields in commit-related helpers without changing the underlying two-stage `committed` -> `done` lifecycle model.
Ensure any response-level workflow cues are derived from canonical transaction state rather than local assumptions where practical.
Add or update regression tests so tool responses are validated against persisted transaction state and expected follow-up behavior.
notes: This ticket is about contract clarity for agents, not about changing the lifecycle so that commit automatically implies terminal success.
Backward compatibility matters: existing fields such as `sha`, `message`, and top-level success indicators should be preserved while richer workflow guidance is added.
The main workflow goal is to make it obvious to an agent that `committed` is still non-terminal when `next_action` remains `tx.end.done`.
